user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the car, including the bumper and trunk area, is clearly visible, allowing for assessment of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows severe damage, including significant dents and broken parts, on the rear bumper and trunk area of the car, supporting the claim of a dent on the back of the car.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows the front bumper and the severe damage on it, allowing for assessment.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 shows severe damage to the front bumper, including a large scrape, dent, and a broken part, which supports the presence of a 'mark' or 'scratch' as claimed, though the actual damage is much more severe. Image 2 shows a different car (Jaguar) with no damage, which is not relevant to the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows the windshield with extensive damage, allowing the claimed crack to be assessed.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows the windshield with severe shattering and cracks, supporting the claim of a crack. However, image 2 shows an undamaged windshield, which is a significant inconsistency requiring manual review.
